data.body:
[G4 child-consistency — container review of parent 1f19-adf2-ffb2-4b09 paired with THIS child (S4 30b3: Attested signing); independent re-confirmation against current source + the actual dependency graph]

Re-ran the 7 cross-child interaction modes for this child against the WHOLE sibling roster (S1 6d56, S2 70c7, S3 fa49, S5 b7fa, S6 1ba5) and re-verified every code claim by reading attest.py and checking each sibling's file_impact. ONE high-confidence finding (dependency gap, mode 3) STANDS; the other 6 modes are clean under the high-confidence / benign-reading filter.

FINDING (dependency gap — the pinned-SHA snapshot basis is not routed into the plan-review attestation hashing path; the S3↔S4 seam owns it by name in neither)
- finding: This child's AC5 requires plan-review's file_impact hashing AND the claim-gate freshness re-check to resolve the ref through ONE shared boundary at the SAME pinned-SHA basis. But all three sign/verify-side hashing sites in plan_review/attest.py still root on the MUTABLE working tree via _config.repo_root, and no child (neither S3, which scopes its re-rooting to the gate file tools, nor this child, whose "In" scope claims the claim-gate ref-coherence but whose file_impact omits attest.py) routes the snapshot handle into that path. The hand-off sits in a seam neither owns.
- criteria: this child (30b3) AC5 ("Plan-review's file_impact hashing and the claim-gate freshness re-check resolve the ref through ONE shared boundary / same pinned-SHA basis; negative test asserts the two gates hash at the SAME ref"); parent AC1 ("all read a faithful snapshot materialized at the pinned SHA") and AC2 (signed verdict reproducible over identical content).
- location: this child (30b3) AC5 + Scope ("Out: ... gate parameter threading (S3)") + file_impact (transition.py, completion.py); S3 fa49 What/Scope/file_impact (completion.py, pai_tools.py, mcp_server.py).
- evidence (verified against current source):
  1. The SIGNED dependency map is hashed from the working tree: dependency_hashes() at attest.py:106-107 — `base = str(_config.repo_root(repo_root))`; `{p: _hash_file(p, base=base) ...}` (docstring line 92: "hashed from the working tree"). This map is signed via sign_plan_review() at attest.py:186-188.
  2. The progressive-refresh _rehash() roots on the mutable checkout: attest.py:191-196 — docstring "Re-hash the given dependency paths at CURRENT working-tree content"; `base = str(_config.repo_root(repo_root))`.
  3. The ADR-0002 claim-gate freshness re-check roots on the mutable checkout: attest.py:325-326 — `base = str(_config.repo_root(repo_root))`; `_hash_file(p, base=base) != digest`. (Whole-HEAD fallback at attest.py:338 likewise resolves through _config.repo_root.) Three sign/verify-side hashing sites, all on the mutable checkout, none reading a snapshot dir.
  4. S3 (fa49) re-roots only "the gate's file tools (pai_tools root / cfg.repo_path)"; its file_impact is completion.py / pai_tools.py / mcp_server.py — NOT attest.py. S3's snapshot re-rooting does not reach attest.py.
  5. This child's file_impact is transition.py + completion.py — also NOT attest.py — yet AC5 (the attest.py hashing path) is in this child's "In" scope while it pushes "gate parameter threading (S3)" out of scope.
  6. Roster-wide file_impact cross-check (all six tickets): S1 rebar_reconciler/pai_tools.py; S2 _engine; S3 completion.py/pai_tools.py/mcp_server.py; S4 transition.py/completion.py; S5 mcp_server.py/_commands; S6 docs/README. NO sibling lists src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py (only docs/ADR/janitor-report prose mention it). The snapshot-handle/pinned-SHA hand-off INTO the attestation hashing path is owned by neither S3 nor explicitly by this child.
- scenarios: attested plan-review signs file_impact hashes; later the claim-gate freshness re-check (attest.py:325-326) re-hashes the same paths from the server working tree via _config.repo_root — possibly a different branch / dirty — yielding spurious "stale-code" drift OR a re-hash that silently passes against the WRONG branch, re-introducing exactly the moving-checkout false-verdict this epic exists to kill. AC5's "guarding against whole-HEAD divergence" is unsatisfiable while attest.py resolves through _config.repo_root.
- impact: AC5 (ref-coherence) and parent AC1/AC2 (snapshot-only reads, reproducibility) are unsatisfiable as decomposed: the resolved-SHA snapshot handle is not routed into dependency_hashes() / _rehash() / the ADR-0002 freshness re-check, and that routing sits in a seam between this child and S3 that neither claims in file_impact.
- checklist_item: dependency gap (cross-child mode 3)
- suggested_fix: Make the snapshot-handle/pinned-SHA basis an explicit hand-off — either (a) extend S3's re-rooting scope to include src/rebar/llm/plan_review/attest.py (dependency_hashes + _rehash + the ADR-0002 freshness re-check), threading the snapshot dir as the hash base in place of _config.repo_root, and add attest.py to S3's file_impact; or (b) add attest.py to THIS child's file_impact plus an explicit AC ("dependency_hashes(), _rehash(), and the freshness re-check hash from the pinned-SHA snapshot, not _config.repo_root"), since AC5 already claims claim-gate ref-coherence as in-scope here. The negative test AC5 demands cannot be authored until one child names attest.py.

Clean on the other 6 modes:
- implicit shared state: verified_at_sha (resolved by S1, threaded by S3, recorded/signed here, surfaced by S5, documented by S6) — consistent producer→consumer chain; no value produced-without-consumer or consumed-without-producer.
- conflicting assumptions: local-mode unsigned (S3 marks-unsigned AC4 / this child bars-signing AC3 / S5 reflects-unsigned / S6 documents) and attested-default-signs (S5 AC2, this child AC1) agree; no contradiction.
- scope overlap: local-mode split (S3 marks-unsigned vs this child enforces-the-bar) is a clean reader/enforcer split — no double-implementation. in-toto Statement: this child BUILDS the envelope (AC4), S6 DOCUMENTS it (this child's What names the S6 hand-off; S6 AC4 claims it). No overlap.
- ordering violation: CLEAN — and the encoded deps are NOT empty (correcting older comments on this ticket): the graph is S1→{S2,S3}, S3→{S4(this),S5}, and S4 & S5 → S6. This child is blocked-by S3 and blocks S6, which is COHERENT with the producer→consumer flow (S3 threads the ref before this child signs; this child builds the in-toto shape before S6 documents it). The dependency edges, where present, point the benign way; no ordering contradiction.
- consumer impact: this child's verified_at_sha record + in-toto shape are consumed by S5 (surfaces) and S6 (documents), both claiming the obligation; no broken downstream contract.
- residual references: --force-close no-signature signal, ADR 0002, the S6 HMAC-trust-model delegation, and the in-toto/DSSE/sigstore forward-path all resolve to real producers/trackers.

Conclusion: one high-confidence cross-child inconsistency (dependency gap, mode 3) stands — re-confirmed against current source (three hashing sites: attest.py:106-107, :191-196, :325-326, all rooted on _config.repo_root) and the actual dependency graph. The remaining 6 modes are clean.
